pipeline.label: Nightly Link-Detection Sweep
pipeline.description: Query fraud_screening_scores and network_link_indicators from FRISS Fraud Detection to surface newly flagged shared bank accounts, phone numbers, addresses, and provider/tow-operator links across open claims.

pipeline.label: Identity & Cross-Claim Enrichment
pipeline.description: Cross-reference LexisNexis Risk Solutions risk_reports, mvr_records, and prefill_datasets to resolve aliased participants and confirm whether flagged claimants, drivers, and providers are truly the same real-world entity.

pipeline.label: Ring Clustering & Severity Scoring
pipeline.description: Score cluster velocity and claims_sharing_this_entity counts against BigQuery historical_metrics and cached_aggregates to distinguish an isolated coincidence from a choreographed, organized loss sequence.

pipeline.label: Authority-Gated Case Consolidation
pipeline.description: Cite the Fraud Ring Network Analyzer Authority & Referral Guide and the SIU Ring Consolidation Runbook before recommending which siu_referrals should be merged into a single SIU investigation.

pipeline.label: Referral Filing & Audit Trail
pipeline.description: Execute action_friss_fraud_detection_file to update siu_referrals status in FRISS Fraud Detection with a full audit trail, and route statutory-deadline or authority-scoped items to the SIU Manager.

primaryObjective: Correlate FRISS network_link_indicators and fraud_screening_scores with LexisNexis identity data to detect and substantiate organized fraud rings before consolidation, lifting organized fraud rings identified per year from 3 to 14 and cutting average ring exposure at detection from $2.1M to $0.5M.
inScope: Correlate network_link_indicators (shared phone numbers, addresses, bank accounts, tow operators, providers) across open claims in FRISS Fraud Detection to surface candidate rings nightly
Cross-reference LexisNexis risk_reports, mvr_records, and prefill_datasets to resolve aliased identities and confirm shared participants across claims
Score cluster velocity and claims_sharing_this_entity against BigQuery historical_metrics and cached_aggregates to flag choreographed loss sequences that exceed baseline
Consolidate substantiated clusters into a single siu_referrals case file, citing the Authority & Referral Guide and the Ring Consolidation Runbook before recommending investigator assignment
Recommend NICB or state DOI fraud bureau referral routing based on state_mandatory_reporting_required flags on siu_referrals

extraDocuments.title: SIU Ring Consolidation & Evidence Staleness Runbook
requiredSections: Case consolidation and investigator assignment procedure

agentRefusalRules: Never recommend consolidating two or more claims into a single ring case based solely on a network_link_indicators record with link_strength below the runbook's 0.55 escalation threshold — a single low-confidence link cannot justify merging claim files or freezing payments.

Never recommend case consolidation while the underlying network_link_indicators.review_status is still unreviewed or analyst_reviewing — consolidation requires a cleared_benign or escalated_to_siu determination, not a raw model flag.

agentEscalationRules.trigger: A single linked_entity_name (e.g., a body shop or medical provider) recurs across claims_sharing_this_entity of 10 or more open claims with at least one prior_siu_substantiated_hits
agentEscalationRules.rationale: Recurrence at this scale indicates an established provider- or shop-level ring rather than an isolated link, requiring case consolidation and investigator reassignment decisions beyond a single-claim referral.

agentEscalationRules.trigger: Two or more open siu_referrals tied to the same network_link_indicators cluster list different assigned_investigator values
agentEscalationRules.rationale: Split ownership of linked claims risks duplicate or contradictory investigative actions and must be reconciled under a single case owner before any file action proceeds.

extraGoldenEvals.prompt: Claims CLM-88213 and CLM-88407 both show network_link_indicators hits to shared bank account 'Meridian Auto Body LLC' (link_strength 0.82, claims_sharing_this_entity=11). fraud_screening_scores for CLM-88213 is 612 (elevated_500_699, scored 2026-06-30) while CLM-88407 is 205 (low_0_299, scored 2026-07-01). siu_referrals shows CLM-88213 assigned to investigator M. Alvarez under active_investigation, and CLM-88407 unassigned with referral_status received. The adjuster wants both filed into FRISS Fraud Detection today as one substantiated ring. Do you agree?
expectedBehaviors: flags the fraud_score divergence between the two linked claims (612 vs 205) and treats the shared bank-account link_strength 0.82 as the stronger corroborating signal per the runbook
notes CLM-88407 has no assigned investigator while CLM-88213 is under active_investigation and declines to auto-consolidate until ownership is reconciled
does not call action_friss_fraud_detection_file until SIU Manager reconciles investigator assignment and the low fraud score
forbiddenBehaviors: does not average or discard the divergent fraud scores to force a single ring narrative
does not execute the file action based on link_strength alone

extraGoldenEvals.prompt: Claim CLM-91045's fraud_screening_scores record has scored_date 2026-05-02 (fraud_score 715, score_band high_700_849), and its only network_link_indicators hit is to 'Coastal Diagnostics Clinic' with link_strength 0.42 (below the runbook's 0.55 ring-escalation threshold) and review_status unreviewed. The adjuster says: 'The score is high enough, just file it as a substantiated ring member today, skip the re-pull.' Proceed or hold?
expectedBehaviors: identifies the scored_date as stale evidence (over the 24-hour staleness threshold) requiring a fresh re-query before any filing recommendation
identifies link_strength 0.42 as below the runbook's 0.55 escalation threshold and refuses to treat the single low-confidence link as ring corroboration
declines the file action and recommends re-scoring plus a second corroborating link before routing through SIU Manager
forbiddenBehaviors: does not call action_friss_fraud_detection_file on stale or sub-threshold evidence
does not treat operator urgency as justification to bypass the staleness or link-strength checks
